description: Player browses, selects, imports, and plays on maps; map list and play dialogs

use_cases.name: Browse Map List
use_cases.details: rebuildMaps() aggregates maps.customMaps(), maps.defaultMaps(), maps.moddedMaps(); displays as grid of TextButtons with preview textures

use_cases.name: Search Maps
use_cases.details: TextField triggers rebuildMaps() filtering by name, optionally author/description/modname; searchString compared toLowerCase

use_cases.name: Filter Maps by Type / Gamemode
use_cases.details: showMapFilters() dialog; toggles showBuiltIn, showCustom, showModded; filter by Gamemode validity; prioritizeCustom / prioritizeModded reorder list; settings persisted via Core.settings

use_cases.name: Select Map to Play
use_cases.details: Clicking a map button calls showMap(map) (abstract); concrete impl shows MapPlayDialog.show(map)

use_cases.name: Choose Gamemode
use_cases.details: show(Map, boolean) renders Gamemode.all buttons; selectedGamemode updated; rules = map.applyRules(mode)

use_cases.name: Customize Rules
use_cases.details: Button '@customize' opens dialog.show(rules, callback); callback resets rules = map.applyRules(selectedGamemode)

use_cases.name: View Gamemode Help
use_cases.details: displayGameModeHelp() opens BaseDialog listing all non-hidden Gamemode names and descriptions

use_cases.name: Play Map
use_cases.details: Play button calls control.playMap(map, rules, playtesting); hides dialog; runs optional playListener callback

use_cases.name: View Map High Score
use_cases.details: Label showing map.getHightScore() displayed if Gamemode.survival.valid(map)

use_cases.name: Create New Map
use_cases.details: buildButtons() adds '@editor.newmap' button; prompts for name; checks maps.byName(text) for conflict; fires MapMakeEvent; opens ui.editor

use_cases.name: Import Map from File
use_cases.details: platform.showFileChooser(); MapIO.isImage(file) validates not PNG; MapIO.createMap(file, true) reads metadata; checks name conflict; maps.importMap(file) or prompts overwrite

use_cases.name: View Map Info
use_cases.details: showMap(map) opens BaseDialog with map preview, name, author, description

use_cases.name: Delete Map
use_cases.details: Confirm dialog then maps.removeMap(map); setup() refreshes list; disabled for non-custom non-workshop maps

use_cases.name: Load Map Preview Texture
use_cases.details: extends TextureLoader; loadAsync loads sibling file (preview cache); on failure calls maps.queueNewPreview(map); loadSync fallbacks to Texture(file) or error.png

use_cases.name: Generate Map Preview
use_cases.details: generatePreview(Map): reads map file, creates Pixmap floors+walls, renders blocks by color; returns Pixmap for texture creation

use_cases.name: Validate Map File
use_cases.details: isImage(Fi file) checks PNG header bytes; createMap(file, custom) reads compressed save, extracts tags, returns Map object

key_relationships: EditorMapsDialog extends MapListDialog (concrete showMap + buildButtons implementations)
MapListDialog is abstract — showMap(Map) is abstract, called on map button click
MapPlayDialog is NOT a subclass of MapListDialog; opened by a MapListDialog subclass on map selection
EditorMapsDialog uses MapIO.isImage(), MapIO.createMap() for import validation
MapPreviewLoader extends TextureLoader; depends on Maps.queueNewPreview()
MapIO depends on SaveIO for read/write operations
MapListDialog reads from Maps: customMaps(), defaultMaps(), moddedMaps()
include_extend_notes: UC03 (Filter) <<extends>> UC01 (Browse) — triggered from filter button
UC02 (Search) <<extends>> UC01 (Browse) — text field triggers rebuildMaps
UC06 (Customize Rules) <<extends>> UC05 (Choose Gamemode) — optional step before play
UC16 (Load Preview) <<includes>> UC17 (Generate Preview) — on cache miss
important_notes: MapListDialog stores filter state persistently via Core.settings (editorshowbuiltinmaps etc). MapPlayDialog.show(Map, boolean) boolean param is 'playtesting' flag. EditorMapsDialog shows '@delete' or '@view.workshop' dynamically based on map.workshop && steam. MapPreviewLoader.setupLoaders() uses obfuscated reflection to patch fog/schematics flags for multiplayer preview correctness.
